name: Sourdough (Tc) Pizza Dough
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Water, if and as necessary
recipeInstructions:
1. Watch the dough carefully and adjust the amount of water or flour if necessary.
2. Top with your favorite toppings.
3. Preheat oven to 500°F for at least 30 minutes (or turn on the oven at the same time you start the bread machine). If using a pan, preheat for at least 1 hour; if using a stone, preheat for at least 1 hour.
4. Grease the pizza pan with olive oil or spray it with a nonstick vegetable spray. Optionally, dust the pan lightly with cornmeal to further prevent sticking (it adds crunch and flavor).
5. While the dough is rising, prepare your toppings and grate cheese; set aside.
6. After the dough has risen once, roll it out on a lightly floured or cornmeal-covered surface (cornmeal preferred) or handstretch the dough into a circle.
7. Pick up the dough and turn over, rolling or stretching from the center outwards until a circle is formed. It may not be a perfect circle, which adds to its uniqueness.
8. Dust the dough only as needed to prevent sticking with flour or cornmeal. Thinner rolls result in thinner crusts; thicker rolls yield thicker dough.
9. Place the dough on the prepared pizza pan or peel. Fold the dough up around the edges of the pan so a slight lip is formed, and crimp the edges.
10. For a thin, crispy crust, place toppings directly on the pizza and bake immediately. For a thicker, chewier crust, let the dough rise for 15 to 30 minutes (longer rises yield thicker dough). Add toppings after this second rising and then bake.
11. The toppings should be placed so that the rim remains unadorned (about 1/4 to 1/2 inch border is ideal) to prevent spillage during baking and provides a nice crust edge to hold when eating.
12. Place the pizza on the bottom rack of the oven or directly on the pizza stone and bake until the crust is golden brown and cheese melted. A preheated oven should take about 5 to 10 minutes for a thin crust.
Precooked Shells:
1. Make extra dough, roll it out, place on the pan/screen/peel, brush with olive oil (or melted garlic butter), and pierce every 1/2 inch or so with a fork.
2. Bake in a preheated 500°F oven for about 5 minutes. Cool, stack with waxed paper between layers, wrap well in plastic, and freeze up to one month.
3. To use, remove dough from freezer (thaw not necessary), brush with oil to prevent sogginess, top as desired, and bake in a preheated 450°F oven for 15 to 20 minutes.
Excess dough can be formed into bread sticks, brushed lightly with water, sprinkled with kosher salt, poppy seeds, or sesame seeds, and baked beside the pizza. Other successful options include salt and vinegar, garlic powder, or a little cayenne.
